Every plant is a working machine

Six parts, each with its own job — from the roots drinking water underground to the fruit guarding tomorrow's seeds. Tap a glowing label to jump in.

① Below the ground

Roots — the anchor & the straw

The root is the part of the plant that stays under the soil. Tiny hair-like branches grip the earth and drink up water.

Two main root systems: many thin fibrous roots vs. one deep tap root.
What roots do
  • Fix the plant firmly in the soil
  • Absorb water and mineral salts through root hairs
  • Store food in some plants — carrot, radish, beetroot, turnip
  • Reproduce — new plants can grow from some roots
Two main types — flip to see examples
🌾

Fibrous Root

tap to flip

Many equal roots

from the base of the stem — no single main root.
Grass, wheat, rice, onion

🥕

Tap Root

tap to flip

One deep main root

with smaller side (lateral) roots.
Bean, balsam, mustard, mango

🥕 Storage roots — roots we eat

In some plants the root itself swells up and fills with stored food, becoming thick and fleshy. These are called storage roots. Because they are packed with food, we eat them as vegetables.

Examples: carrot, radish, beetroot and turnip. Remember — these are true roots, unlike potato and ginger which are really stems.

Special underground parts

Some swollen underground parts look like roots but are really modified stems!

  • Tuber — a swollen tip that stores food, with buds called "eyes". Potato
  • Rhizome — a thick stem that grows sideways underground. Ginger, lotus
Potato tubers and ginger rhizomes store food underground.
💡Olympiad trap: Potato and ginger are stems, not roots — even though they grow underground! A true root that stores food is the carrot or sweet potato.
🎯 Game: drag each into the right box

🥕 Storage Root

🥔 Stem Tuber

🫚 Rhizome

Quick check: Which plant has a tap root system?
② The main highway

Stem — support & transport

The stem is the main part of the shoot above the ground. It holds the plant upright and carries water and food between every part.

The stem connects roots to leaves — water rises, food travels down.
Parts of the stem
  • Node — where leaves attach
  • Internode — gap between two nodes
  • Petiole — the stalk joining a leaf to the stem
  • Buds — at the tip (apical) and at nodes (axillary)
Functions
  • Holds the plant upright; bears leaves, buds, flowers, fruits
  • Carries water & minerals up through the xylem
  • Carries food down through the phloem
  • Stores food — sugarcane, potato, ginger
⚙️ See the transport tubes work — tap a button
🌱 Explore the stem — tap a part or a chip
Tap a yellow dot 👆
Each part of the stem has a special job. Explore them all!
💡Did you know? A bud is a baby shoot waiting to grow. An axillary bud sits in the angle between a leaf and the stem, and it can grow into a new branch or a flower.
Quick check: Which tube carries food made in the leaves to the rest of the plant?

How a leaf makes food

Inside the leaf are tiny green packets called chloroplasts, full of the green pigment chlorophyll. Chlorophyll traps sunlight, and the leaf uses it with water and air to make food. That is why the leaf is called the plant's food factory or kitchen.

Breathing & cooling

The leaf surface has tiny holes called stomata. Each hole (a stoma) is guarded by two guard cells. Tap the button to watch them open and close.

When closed, the guard cells press together and the pore is shut.

Nucleus Chloroplasts Vacuole Guard cell Cell wall
⚡ Watch photosynthesis happen — press play
Sunlight + Water + Carbon dioxide  →  Food + Oxygen
💡Did you know? Leaves are green only because of chlorophyll. In autumn the chlorophyll fades and the hidden orange and red colours show through!
Quick check: The tiny holes a leaf breathes through are called…
④ The reproductive star

Flower — beauty with a purpose

Flowers are the most colourful part of a plant. Their bright petals and sweet smell attract insects — and they help the plant make seeds. Explore the parts, then try Label it! mode.

Tap a dot to learn each part — or switch to Label it! mode and drag the labels on.
Tap a pink dot ↑
Discover what each flower part does.
🎯 Match each part to its job

Part

Job

💡Did you know? The sepals are the small green leaf-like parts that wrapped and protected the flower while it was still a bud.
Quick check: Which part protects the flower while it is still a bud?
⑤ Tomorrow's plants

Fruits & Seeds

A fruit forms from a flower and protects the seeds inside. Each seed holds a tiny baby plant and its packed lunch.

Inside a seed: a protective seed coat and food-filled cotyledons.
Inside a seed
  • Seed coat (testa) — the tough outer cover that protects the seed
  • Cotyledons — store food for the baby plant
Two seed types — flip to compare
🫘

Dicot

tap to flip

Two cotyledons

splits into two halves.
Bean, mango, pea

🌽

Monocot

tap to flip

One cotyledon

stays in one piece.
Corn, wheat, rice

🎯 Game: sort the fruits by how many seeds

1 One Seed

🔢 Many Seeds

🚫 Seedless

How seeds travel — dispersal

Fruits help scatter seeds far from the parent plant so they have room to grow:

  • 🌬️ Wind — light, winged seeds float away
  • 💧 Water — a coconut floats across the sea to a new shore
  • 🐾 Animals — tasty fruits are eaten and seeds dropped elsewhere
💡Did you know? A mango has just one seed, a papaya has many, and a banana has none — it is seedless!
Quick check: A corn seed has how many cotyledons?
⑥ Round and round

The Life Cycle of a Plant

Every part you've met now works together. Tap a stage, or press play to watch a seed become a tree and start all over again.

🌰Seedbaby plant inside, waiting
🌱Sproutwith water, air & warmth it wakes up
🌿Seedlingroots dig down, shoot reaches up
🪴Young Plantleaves make food
🌳Mature Plantstrong stem and many leaves
🌸Flowersattract insects, get pollinated
🍎Fruit & Seedseeds form — the cycle begins again

Games & Puzzles

Five quick brain games to lock in everything you've learned. Flip, scramble, match, fill, and solve!

Read the question, guess in your head, then tap the card to flip it. Use the arrows for the next card.

Choose the right word from each dropdown to complete the sentence.

🧠 Memory Match

Flip two cards to find matching pairs — a plant part and its job. Find all 6 pairs!
